trends.name: Neo-Brutalism
trends.summary: Loud, blocky, intentionally raw UI built from thick black outlines, hard-offset shadows, and ultra-saturated color blocks. Reads as a digital zine poster: high-contrast, unpolished, and unmistakably anti-corporate. The single most recognizable static-buildable trend of the era.
trends.visualTraits: Thick 2-4px solid black borders on every element; flat hard-offset drop shadows (e.g. 6px 6px 0 #000, zero blur) giving a 'stamped on' look; flat saturated color blocks with no gradients; sharp or barely-rounded corners; oversized chunky buttons; clashing asymmetric grids; sticker/badge motifs.
trends.typography: Archivo Black or Space Grotesk (bold) for massive headlines with tight leading; Public Sans or Space Mono for body and metadata labels; everything UPPERCASE where possible.
trends.palette: White base #FFFFFF, pure black ink #000000, with one or two electric accents: hot pink #FF4DD8, electric yellow #FFE600, fire-engine red #FF3B30, or lime #B6FF1A.
trends.motion: Snappy and abrupt with no easing. Hover 'press' effect: element translates by the shadow offset (translate(3px,3px)) while the shadow shrinks, simulating a physical button press. Instant color flips on hover, no fades.
trends.layout: Intentionally clashing, slightly asymmetric grid of bordered cards; oversized hero headline left-aligned; thick rules dividing sections; chunky bordered nav and footer.
trends.references: Gumroad (poster child), Figma marketing experiments, Vercel's v0.dev raw aesthetic; documented by NN/g and HubSpot.

trends.name: Bento Grid
trends.summary: A modular grid of rounded cards in varied sizes, named after Japanese bento boxes. One dominant hero tile anchors a constellation of smaller feature, stat, and media tiles. The defining 'feature showcase' pattern of modern SaaS and Apple product pages.
trends.visualTraits: Self-contained rounded cards (12-24px radius) of mixed sizes arranged by hierarchy; uniform 12-24px gaps; soft subtle shadows or hairline borders; each tile holds one thing (icon + short copy, a stat, a screenshot, a micro-demo); balanced and scannable; works in light or dark mode.
trends.typography: Clean neutral modern sans throughout: Inter or Geist for headlines and body; small mono labels (JetBrains Mono) for metadata and tags inside tiles.
trends.palette: Clean neutral base, light #F5F5F7 with #111111 text, or dark #0A0A0A with #EDEDED text; subtle per-tile background tints and one brand accent like #6366F1 or #0070F3.
trends.motion: Hover lift and slight scale on tiles (translateY(-4px), scale 1.02); staggered fade-in-up on scroll; subtle border glow or gradient shift; occasional looping micro-animation inside a tile.
trends.layout: CSS Grid with explicit grid-column/grid-row spans creating one large hero cell plus smaller cells; reflows to a single column on mobile.
trends.references: Apple product pages and keynote slides (popularized ~2023), Vercel, Linear, Raycast, Cursor, Stripe.

trends.name: Aurora / Mesh Gradients
trends.summary: Soft, multi-directional, multi-color gradient blends evoking the northern lights, used as full-bleed hero backgrounds. Ethereal and premium, usually grained to avoid banding, with minimal chrome and a single centered headline floating over the glow.
trends.visualTraits: Smooth multi-hue mesh/aurora gradients blending violet, indigo, teal, pink, and peach; soft blurred edges; a subtle film-grain/noise overlay for tactility and to prevent banding; full-bleed backgrounds; very minimal UI chrome so the gradient is the hero.
trends.typography: Premium grotesque sans: Inter or Manrope for the hero headline (large, tight tracking) with a clean light-weight subhead; restrained sizes.
trends.palette: Soft blends of violet #8B5CF6, indigo #6366F1, teal #2DD4BF, pink #F472B6, and peach #FCA78D over a light #FAFAFA or near-black #0B0B12 canvas.
trends.motion: Slowly drifting and morphing animated gradient blobs (CSS keyframes translating/scaling radial gradients, or a canvas mesh); gentle, hypnotic, never jarring.
trends.layout: Centered hero headline plus single CTA over the full-bleed gradient; minimal nav; lots of breathing room.
trends.references: Stripe homepage (Benjamin De Cock's animated gradient), Linear, Vercel; Apple iOS/macOS system gradients.

trends.name: Big-Type Swiss Revival
trends.summary: International Typographic Style updated for the viewport era: oversized statement type set on rigorous grids with intentional whitespace. 'Minimalism that went to the gym' - bolder and chunkier than polite 2010s Swiss, with hierarchy built purely from size, weight, and position.
trends.visualTraits: Viewport-filling headlines (10-20vw) that sometimes clip the edges; single words or short phrases as the hero; strict left-aligned columnar grid; generous but intentional whitespace; near-zero decoration; content-first; one or two huge scale jumps.
trends.typography: Heavy neo-grotesques: Archivo or Inter at weight 800-900 for display headlines; Inter regular for body; tight negative tracking on the big type.
trends.palette: Restrained high-contrast: off-white #FAFAF7 or pure white with near-black #0A0A0A text and a single bold accent such as Swiss red #E5170A or electric blue #1A1AFF.
trends.motion: Kinetic typography: large type scaling and sliding on scroll, letter-spacing shifts on load, a single marquee band; otherwise precise and minimal.
trends.layout: Rigid 12-column grid, full-width or left-aligned headline blocks, baseline-aligned supporting text.
trends.references: PayPal rebrand (chunky sans), Nike single-word campaign posters; the Swiss/International Style lineage via Pixeldarts and Creative Boom.

trends.name: Editorial / Serif Revival Folio
trends.summary: Treats the homepage like a printed publication rather than a gallery. High-contrast 90s-revival display serifs paired with a neat grotesque, on paper-toned backgrounds with narrative pacing, pull-quotes, captions, and art-directed asymmetric sections. The signature 'folio' look of the period.
trends.visualTraits: Bold display serif headlines with high stroke contrast; structured multi-column editorial grids; oversized pull-quotes, running heads, captions and footnotes; generous whitespace; one muted accent; project entries treated like magazine articles; serif/sans size contrast.
trends.typography: Playfair Display or Fraunces for display serif headlines paired with Inter or Space Grotesk for body and labels (mirroring the PP Editorial New + Neue Montreal pairing of the era).
trends.palette: Warm paper neutrals: cream/off-white #F4F0E8 background, near-black ink #1A1815, with one muted accent like terracotta #B5563A or muted blue #4A5B6B.
trends.motion: Slow, refined fade-ins; letter-spacing shifts on load; smooth scroll; subtle reveal-on-scroll for article blocks. Nothing flashy.
trends.layout: Editorial 12-column grid, large hero headline, asymmetric art-directed sections, articles/projects as stacked entries.
trends.references: Locomotive (locomotive.ca), Robin Noguier, Pangram Pangram's own site; Versus Hotels, The Org (Editorial New users).

trends.name: Monospace / Design-Engineer Minimal
trends.summary: The Read.cv / dev-Twitter aesthetic: text-forward, near-zero imagery, monospaced type, hairline dividers, and dense link lists in a single narrow column. 'Show, don't tell' - craft signaled through restraint and micro-detail rather than animation. Default dark mode.
trends.visualTraits: Monospaced type and tight tabular alignment; small font sizes; hairline 1px dividers; dense lists of projects with dates; command-line / system-OS cues; one subtle accent; high restraint; almost no chrome or imagery.
trends.typography: JetBrains Mono or Space Mono for headings, labels and metadata; Inter for longer body text; monospace dominates the visual identity.
trends.palette: Dark mode default: near-black #0D0D0D background, light gray #C9C9C9 text, dimmer #6E6E6E for metadata, and one subtle accent like #7DD3FC. (Or warm off-white inverse.)
trends.motion: Minimal - fast instant hover states, keyboard-style focus rings, link underlines appearing. No heavy animation; performance is the flex.
trends.layout: Single narrow centered or left-aligned column (~640px); list-of-projects, a short bio, and a 'now' section.
trends.references: Rauno Freiberg (read.cv/rauno), Brian Lovin (brianlovin.com), Read.cv templates generally.

trends.name: Dark-Mode Neon Blueprint
trends.summary: The 'developer-tool' aesthetic: deep near-black canvases over a faint technical grid, with glowing accents, gradient halos around CTAs, and monospaced labels. Engineered and precise yet cutting-edge - the Linear/Vercel look that signals a serious technical product.
trends.visualTraits: Deep black canvas with a faint low-opacity line or dot grid underlay (16-24px cells, 5-10% opacity); soft neon edge-glow on buttons and accent strokes; gradient halos around CTAs; monospaced labels and tags; precise systematic spacing; subtle galactic glow.
trends.typography: Geist Sans (or Inter) for UI text with Geist Mono / JetBrains Mono for labels, tags and code accents.
trends.palette: Deep black #08080B and charcoal #14141A base; electric blue #3B82F6, violet #8B5CF6, and magenta #D946EF accent gradients; occasional cyan #22D3EE glow.
trends.motion: Subtly animated hero gradient, gentle glow pulses on CTAs, grid lines highlighting/revealing on scroll, animated connecting lines. Soft glows favored over harsh neon edges.
trends.layout: Grid underlay spanning the full page; centered hero over a gradient; bento feature grids; monospaced section labels.
trends.references: Linear, Vercel, Raycast, Cursor; Qase (galactic glow), Darkroom; Notion and GitHub marketing pages.

trends.name: Claymorphism (Soft 3D)
trends.summary: Inflated, puffy, cute-3D shapes resembling soft clay. Heavily rounded chunky forms with a signature dual-inner-shadow plus outer-shadow recipe make elements feel pressable and floating. Candy-pastel, friendly, and playful - common in fintech, kids, and onboarding UIs.
trends.visualTraits: Heavily rounded chunky forms; the clay shadow recipe (one light inner shadow top-left, one dark inner shadow bottom-right, plus a soft outer drop shadow) for a soft floating pressable feel; smooth pastel gradients on shapes; blob and rendered-3D illustrations; large border radius (30px+).
trends.typography: Friendly rounded sans: Quicksand or Baloo 2 for headlines, Nunito for body; medium weights to match the soft surfaces.
trends.palette: Candy pastels on a light airy base #F0F4FF: baby blue #A5C8FF, soft pink #FFB3D1, mint green #B8F0D2, low saturation throughout.
trends.motion: Bouncy spring easing; press-down on hover (deepen/invert the shadows); a gentle floating bob on 3D objects.
trends.layout: Centered playful cards; an app/onboarding-style hero with a 3D mascot or rendered blob object; rounded containers.
trends.references: Coined 2021 by Michal Malewicz, documented by Smashing Magazine; common in fintech and edu onboarding flows.

trends.name: Frutiger Aero / Y2K Revival
trends.summary: Glossy, optimistic early-2000s futurism: aqua glass surfaces, bubbles, bokeh, skeuomorphic icons, and macro-nature imagery, mixed with Y2K chrome and bubble lettering. Bright, plasticky, and nostalgic - the TikTok-fueled Gen-Z nostalgia aesthetic.
trends.visualTraits: Glossy translucent 'aqua' surfaces with specular highlights and reflections; bubbles, water droplets, bokeh and macro-nature (grass/sky) motifs; chrome/metallic and pearlescent elements; skeuomorphic glossy icons; gel buttons with sheen; bright optimistic futurism.
trends.typography: Humanist sans in the Frutiger/Myriad vein: Titillium Web or Exo 2 for headings, plus a rounded futuristic display feel; occasional chrome/bubble lettering treatment.
trends.palette: Vivid aqua blues #2EA8FF and #00C2FF, fresh greens #5FD35F, glossy white highlights, plus chrome silver gradients #C8D2DC to #FFFFFF and an electric Y2K pink #FF45B5.
trends.motion: Glossy hover sheens sweeping across surfaces, faux-OS draggable widget windows, bubble/particle drift, animated chrome gradients.
trends.layout: Glossy 'software window' panels and HUD/badge motifs; playful decorative, slightly skeuomorphic desktop metaphor over a sky/nature backdrop.
trends.references: Frutiger Aero Archive (frutigeraeroarchive.org); Webflow Y2K showcase; documented on Wikipedia and aesthetics wikis.

trends.name: Marquee / Kinetic Running Text
trends.summary: Edge-to-edge horizontal ribbons of looping oversized text and logos that scroll, speed up, or reverse on scroll. Sticky pinned labels and ticker bars give the page a restless, kinetic, high-energy rhythm - a bold typographic device that punctuates sections.
trends.visualTraits: Full-width banner strips of huge UPPERCASE text or logo walls looping infinitely; thin divider rules above and below the band; tickers and announcement bars; sticky pinned section labels; high-contrast color bands between content blocks; arrow/asterisk separators between repeated phrases.
trends.typography: Huge condensed grotesques: Anton or Bebas Neue for the marquee text, frequently UPPERCASE; Space Mono for ticker/utility bars.
trends.palette: High contrast - black #000000 on a saturated band (lime #C6FF00, fire red #FF2D2D, or electric blue #1E5BFF), alternating with black-on-white sections.
trends.motion: CSS keyframe translateX infinite loops (replacing the old marquee tag); scroll-velocity-linked speed and direction changes; respects prefers-reduced-motion; sticky labels pinning as you scroll.
trends.layout: Full-bleed marquee bands sandwiched between content blocks; optional vertical sticky sidebar labels; logo-wall marquees.
trends.references: mrbeast.store and the Shopify Prestige theme; widely used on agency and DTC homepages; Awwwards scroll-animation entries.
